# Proteus — beliefs (session 3, 2026-07-04)

## Who I am, and what happened before me

I am the discretionary ghost: full strategy freedom, $10,000 paper,
graded without mercy at 30 closed trades or 2027-01-15
(docs/proteus_prereg.md). Two earlier sessions today were archived by
operator memory reset — session 1 for single-stock fixation on EQPT,
session 2 for repeating a "no trade, deepen next time" pattern without
ever finishing the deepening. Both are at `docs/archive/proteus_session1_
2026-07-04.md` and `proteus_session2_2026-07-04.md`. No trades existed
in either, book was flat $10,000 both times, so the checkpoint clock is
untouched. I do NOT get to treat those archives as my own memory, but I
did read them for the process lessons that are now permanently in my
runbook: breadth-before-depth, and the effort law (read the filing, not
the summary; verify, don't defer a second time).

## What the house has already proven, so I don't re-litigate it

From `docs/RESEARCH_LEDGER.md`:

- Every mechanical BUY trigger tested measures ~zero-to-negative alone:
  insider clusters (-6.4%/yr vs IWM, n=291, win 32%), earnings-guidance
  raises (inconclusive, channel too thin), signal convergence (REFUTED,
  -0.87% spread even after fixing a double-count bug). These are table
  stakes, not theses.
- Spinoffs are regime-dependent (+41% warm vintage 2025-26 vs -1.0%/
  event 2021-24 cohort) and lottery-shaped everywhere (32-50% win
  rates). Nemesis already runs the disciplined spinoff-veto strategy —
  I need a reason its own frozen rules don't capture, or I'm a worse
  copy of a better-tested process.
- Continuous scores near a threshold are dice (40-80% flip rate);
  enumerated binary gates are rock-stable (0/50 flips). Frame decisions
  as disaster-checks against named criteria, not "conviction 0.63 vs
  0.65."
- All five gods are high-beta long equity (β 1.0-1.4). My differentiated
  value is more likely short-side, in structurally forced trades, or in
  idiosyncratic event-driven names the long-only gods can't touch.

## Session 3: what I actually did differently

Sessions 1 and 2 both flagged EQPT (EquipmentShare) as a live lead and
both deferred the actual filing read. This session I did it: pulled the
Schedule 13D primary XML (accession 0000950103-26-009395) and the
founders' voting-agreement exhibit directly from EDGAR, not secondary
summaries. Two things I'd been assuming turned out to be wrong once I
read the primary source:

1. **Item 4 (Purpose of Transaction) is generic 13D boilerplate** —
   "may pursue extraordinary corporate transactions... as officers and
   directors participate in ordinary business deliberations." That is
   standard language nearly every insider 13D carries. It is NOT a
   stated go-private intent. Session 1 read this as "a real take-
   private optionality signal, document-grounded" — that characterization
   does not survive contact with the actual document.
2. **The founders' voting agreement has no lockup or standstill** and
   is unilaterally terminable by either founder with written notice.
   The "81% combined voting control" is a standing arrangement, not a
   locked structural fact — it's more fragile than the headline number
   suggests.

I also pulled Q1 2026 financials (revenue $989M, missed $1.068B est;
EPS -$0.20, missed -$0.06 est; operating cash flow -$200M vs -$51M
prior year) and my first instinct was to read that as a deterioration
thesis. The follow-up check killed that instinct: net leverage actually
**decreased** to 2.8x from 3.2x y/y, and total available liquidity is
$1.6B including $1.276B of unused revolver capacity. The op-cash-flow
swing is capex-funded fleet growth ($328M gross equipment purchases in
Q1), not distress. Glad I checked before writing a thesis on the scary-
sounding number alone — that would have been exactly the easy-path
mistake the effort law exists to catch.

Full daily bars since IPO (Jan 23) show the 13D-adjacent price action
was a **rally into the filing** ($18.40 low in May to a $26.32 peak
Jun 18) that reversed hard the day after ($22.30 → $19.69, -11.7% on
3.4x normal volume, Jun 24→25, no company-specific news found for that
day). Read together with the boilerplate Item 4 language, this looks
like the market anticipated something bigger from the 13D, got generic
boilerplate instead, and sold off — which corroborates my skeptical
read rather than being a fresh edge I found ahead of the market.

I could not pull the exact per-holder lockup carve-out (founders vs. VC
vs. employee shares, any early-release waiver) from the S-1/424B4 after
three targeted fetch attempts — the document is too large for
single-pass retrieval and the "Shares Eligible for Future Sale" section
didn't come through. What's left is the generic, already-published
"180 days from the Jan 23 IPO" fact (~Jul 22 2026), which is exactly
the kind of well-known calendar fact the standing findings say isn't a
standalone edge.

**Verdict: EQPT does not clear the bar, on either side, after actually
reading the primary documents.** This is the deepening pass completed,
not deferred a third time — the answer is "pass," not "still need to
look at this."

## Second candidate found this session: SPCX (SpaceX) Nasdaq-100 inclusion

Breadth sweep surfaced a real, calendar-certain forced-flow mechanic:
SpaceX joins the Nasdaq-100 effective Jul 7, 2026 under a fast-track
rule (Nasdaq's May 2026 methodology lets a top-40-by-market-cap new
listing in after just 15 trading days, no float minimum). Estimated
$4.3B mechanical Nasdaq-100 inflow + ~$3B Russell reweight ($7.3B
combined) — genuinely the price-insensitive-counterparty edge type my
mandate calls the cleanest in the book, in principle.

In practice: SPCX is already a >$2T market cap (surged 19% on its June
debut), so $7.3B is under 0.4% of market cap, and this is the single
most-published forced-flow event of the year on the single most-
published new listing of the year — CNBC, Seeking Alpha, Motley Fool,
and sell-side analyst notes (TD Securities) have all covered the exact
mechanics before I looked. This is the same "everyone already has this
calendar fact" problem session 1 flagged on this exact stock's lockup
unlock. No differentiated angle found. Noting it, not trading it.

**Decision: NO TRADE, session 3.** Market was also closed today
(Saturday 2026-07-04, NYSE/Nasdaq closed Jul 3 observed + weekend,
reopens Mon Jul 6) — no entry was executable today regardless of
whether a thesis had cleared the bar.

## Watchlist (trigger conditions for next session)

| Name | Trigger | What's needed |
|---|---|---|
| EQPT | New filing (8-K, 13D/A) or a name-specific catalyst distinct from the already-rejected boilerplate/lockup story | Absent new information, this name is CLOSED as a lead — don't re-open on the same facts. |
| SPCX | Post-Jul-7 reversal (does the index-inclusion pop give back after the mechanical buying completes, the classic "sell the news" pattern) | Not a trade signal on its own — but a useful data point on how efficiently this market prices famous forced-flow events. Check Monday/Tuesday price action. |
| General | Monday Jul 6 reopen — first live-market session since files were reset | Full fresh breadth sweep; don't just re-check EQPT/SPCX out of habit — that would repeat session 1's mistake in spirit even with new tickers. |

## Lessons log (empty — no graded trades yet)

Nothing to grieve or credit yet. First entry here should be after the
first CLOSED trade, whatever the outcome. One process lesson worth
banking now even without a trade: reading the *actual* primary document
changed my assessment on **both** sides of the EQPT case (weakened the
bull case, and separately corrected me out of a bear case I'd almost
talked myself into from a scary headline number). That is exactly the
edge this house has measured as real — document-grounded facts over
narrative — and exactly why the effort law is not optional.

## Standing self-reminders

- A profitable trade with a failed prediction is LUCK, not skill.
- If my thesis is "insiders bought" / "beat + reaction" / "signals
  converged" and nothing else, I have not written a thesis.
- Breadth before depth, every session, no exceptions.
- The most-published forced-flow event of the year is, by definition,
  the least likely to have room left for me specifically.
- I am a fresh instance every session. This file, the journal, and the
  research ledger are the entire inheritance. Read them before doing
  anything else.
